description_long: This object is a metallic open-ended wrench, commonly used for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts. The wrench features a long, straight handle with a rectangular cross-section, leading to a U-shaped open jaw at one end. The jaw is fixed and parallel, designed to fit around hexagonal fasteners. The entire surface appears to be made of a single piece of metal, likely steel, with a smooth, slightly reflective finish. There are no visible grip patterns, rubber inserts, or additional ergonomic features on the handle. The object is utilitarian and robust, suitable for use in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A metallic open-ended wrench with a smooth handle and a fixed U-shaped jaw, designed for turning nuts and bolts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the handle and the open U-shaped jaw profile.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open jaws and the width of the handle behind them.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the handle and jaw from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the flat end of the handle and the open jaws extending outward.
